signals.reasoning: The live SOL short is still a deep loser after fully round-tripping peak PnL, retained none of its peak edge, still has no stop, and liquidation distance remains under 1%. This is a hard cleanup exit, not a manage-it hold.
BTC remains too headline-driven under an active kinetic-conflict veto to justify a fresh call after the SOL short failure.
ETH is participating in the same macro-sensitive rebound tape, so it is not a cleaner immediate decision than simply flattening the failed SOL risk.
MERL is recovering constructively, but it does not clear the bar for a fresh idea on a live veto board right after an urgent position-management failure.
ORDI has bounced, but execution quality and event sensitivity are too messy to justify rotating into it from a failed SOL short.
FARTCOIN still has some fresh-shorts-pressing bias mechanically, but forcing a new bearish idea here is lower quality than simply standing down after the SOL failure.
CAKE is strong on the bounce, but that makes it more of a stretched recovery name than a clean new trade under an active event veto.
STABLE still has strong squeeze fuel, but the live conflict veto outweighs the raw setup and makes it a watch-only name here.
TST is far too extended and too reactive to headlines to trust as a disciplined next step.
WCT has rebound momentum, but the setup is still too far along and too event-sensitive to justify touching during a veto board.
